commands.review_summary:
The branch adds cooperative yielding and abort checks to Ollama native NDJSON stream processing, plus dense-stream timer and abort regression tests.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +86. Total +133 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the scoped Ollama native-stream path: current main processes dense parsed NDJSON chunks without an in-loop cooperative yield, and the PR adds focused regression tests for timer progress and abort classification. No for the full linked Windows/local-provider cluster, which the PR explicitly did not rerun end to end.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the maintainer-labeled member PR needs a human landing decision and should remain scoped to the Ollama mitigation.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- ports the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yields and checks aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- preserves caller cancellation semantics during dense stream processing and adds regression coverage for both timer progress and abort classification

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop, while caller aborts still surface as `aborted` rather than provider `error`.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate on the maintained branch before the latest clean rebase, focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree after the latest rebase, formatter/lint/diff checks, and branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: focused local wrapper passed `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` with 12/12 tests after the final rebase to `d649548a7a2`; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.83)`. AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed on the refreshed branch with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_8fe4c2ecf3b1`, slug `quick-barnacle`, run `run_c06de346f1af`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `extensions` and `extensionTests`.

Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, aborts during dense processing keep `reason` and assistant `stopReason` set to `aborted`, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. The AWS Crabbox changed gate was not rerun after the latest clean rebase because the patch stayed scoped to the same two Ollama files; this is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
target.author: vincentkoc
